subject: Computer Networking
title: Bluetooth Basics
description: A micro-lesson that makes Bluetooth Basics usable.

hook.text: Your headphones, keyboard, and smartwatch all talk to your phone wirelessly. They all use Bluetooth.

buildup.text: Bluetooth operates at 2.4 GHz over short range — typically under 10 meters. Low power is its defining feature.

discovery.text: Devices pair by exchanging keys. Once paired, they reconnect automatically whenever they're in range.

twist.text: Classic Bluetooth and BLE are different. BLE uses far less power and is designed for IoT sensors and wearables.

climax.text: Bluetooth 5 quadrupled the range and doubled the speed of BLE. The latest versions add direction finding too.

punchline.text: Bluetooth connects the things closest to you. Short range, big impact.

quiz.question: What is BLE designed for?
options: Low-power devices like sensors and wearables
